record.title: The Iron Horse Consecutive Games Streak
record.text: Lou Gehrig appeared in 2,130 consecutive regular-season games for the New York Yankees, a record that stood for decades and defined his durability.
record.summary: Canonical pilot story for Gehrig's consecutive-games streak.
record.search_text: iron horse consecutive games streak lou gehrig yankees durability
record.credit_line: National Baseball Hall of Fame — Lou Gehrig player file summary.

record.title: Press Coverage of Gehrig's Streak
record.text: Contemporary newspaper accounts emphasized Gehrig's reliability and contrasted his everyday presence with the injuries that sidelined other stars.
record.summary: Alternate perspective on the same tag as the canonical streak story.
record.search_text: press coverage gehrig streak contemporary newspaper reliability
record.credit_line: Library of Congress — historical newspaper collection.

record.title: Gehrig at Yankee Stadium
record.text: A portrait-era photograph captures Lou Gehrig in Yankees uniform during the late 1920s home schedule.
record.summary: Pilot story with an approved photo association for media search and rendering checks.
record.search_text: gehrig yankee stadium portrait photograph uniform 1928

record.title: Farewell Address at Yankee Stadium
record.text: On July 4, 1939, Lou Gehrig delivered his farewell address at Yankee Stadium, thanking teammates, family, and fans while facing ALS.
record.summary: Event-year pilot story for anniversary and year-browse verification.
record.search_text: farewell address yankee stadium july 4 1939 als luckiest man
record.credit_line: Major League Baseball — historical event summary.
